# data-wrapper

data-wrapper is a zero-dependency, HTML-first reactivity library built around
one custom element: `<data-wrapper>`. A view is an HTML file with an optional
module script. The framework has exactly three binding tokens: `$` writes DOM
properties and attributes, `*` runs structural directives, and `@` listens for
events. There is no JSX, virtual DOM, compiler, or text interpolation.

Canonical docs:

- Framework guide: https://data-wrapper.org/framework
- Technical info: https://data-wrapper.org/info
- Framework comparison: https://data-wrapper.org/compare
- Live examples: https://data-wrapper.org/examples
- Tutorials: https://data-wrapper.org/tutorials
- Full AI reference: https://data-wrapper.org/llms-full.txt
- Agent authoring guide: https://data-wrapper.org/agent.md

Core facts:

- Install with the framework script, then mount a view:
  `<script src="https://data-wrapper.org/dist/data-wrapper.min.js"></script>`
  and `<data-wrapper src="/views/example.html"></data-wrapper>`.
- Views may contain one `<script type="module" data-module="...">`.
- Built-in directives are exactly `*list`, `*if`, and `*src`.
- Bindings are attributes. Do not invent `{{ interpolation }}`, `*for`,
  `*each`, `*match`, `bind:value`, or inline expression syntax.
- Named module exports are shared component state. A default export factory
  creates per-wrapper instance state.
- Use `action()` or `flush()` after mutations so active bindings update.
- Loaded view URLs are trusted code and must remain same-origin.
- Multi-page projects use a physical `index.html` document per route plus a
  shared layout view composed through `*src`. A client-side router is only
  justified for stateful app screens, not a finite set of content pages.
